sectionSubtitle: Six AI signal types — each engineered through the CSL gate pipeline, validated against historical data, and published with full performance attribution.
disclaimer: All signals are informational and AI-generated. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

signalTypes.name: Momentum
signalTypes.description: Identifies assets exhibiting statistically significant directional persistence across multiple timeframes (1h, 4h, 1D). The CSL AMPLIFY gate strengthens momentum signals where price rate-of-change, volume flow, and relative strength all converge above the φ-scaled THRESHOLD gate cutoff. Momentum signals include a directional bias (long/short), a CSL confidence score (0–1), and φ-scaled entry, stop, and target levels.
signalTypes.latency: < 50ms from data receipt to signal publication

signalTypes.name: Mean Reversion
signalTypes.description: Detects statistically oversold or overbought conditions using φ-scaled Bollinger Bands, z-score deviations from rolling means, and RSI extremes. The CSL SUPPRESS gate filters mean-reversion candidates when trend strength is too high (preventing reversal trades against strong trends). Entry triggers fire when price re-enters the φ-derived equilibrium zone.
signalTypes.frequency: Real-time, updated every 5 minutes for intraday, hourly for swing

signalTypes.name: CSL Consensus
signalTypes.description: The highest-conviction signal type — published only when all applicable CSL gate types (THRESHOLD, AMPLIFY, ROUTE, MERGE) reach simultaneous agreement across at least three independent signal inputs. CSL Consensus signals represent the highest CSL confidence scores (typically > 0.85) and the tightest risk parameters, making them the preferred signal type for larger position sizing.
signalTypes.frequency: Event-driven — published only when consensus is achieved, typically 2–8 times per day per asset

signalTypes.name: Phi Breakout
signalTypes.description: Identifies price breakouts through key φ-derived Fibonacci extension levels (0.618, 1.0, 1.618, 2.618, 4.236) with volume confirmation above the φ-scaled baseline average. The CSL ROUTE gate assigns the breakout a directional bias, and the SPLIT gate generates symmetric long/short signals when the breakout occurs at a significant Fibonacci confluence zone.
signalTypes.frequency: Real-time, updated every 1 minute for intraday breakouts

signalTypes.name: Sentiment Aggregate
signalTypes.description: Aggregates sentiment from financial news, SEC filings, earnings call transcripts, social media (X/Twitter, Reddit r/wallstreetbets and institutional subs), and options flow data through NLP sentiment models. The CSL MERGE gate combines all sentiment sources into a unified directional bias score, with the SUPPRESS gate dampening signals when sentiment extremes historically precede reversals.
signalTypes.frequency: Updated every 10 minutes (news-driven), real-time for options flow
signalTypes.latency: < 200ms (NLP processing adds latency vs. pure technical signals)

signalTypes.name: Volatility Surface
signalTypes.description: Models the full implied volatility surface across strikes and expirations for equity options, identifying mispricings, volatility skew shifts, and term structure anomalies that present statistical trading opportunities. Powered by a calibrated Heston stochastic volatility model with φ-scaled mean-reversion speed and vol-of-vol parameters. Signals include IV rank, IV percentile, and recommended options strategy structures.
signalTypes.frequency: Updated at every option chain refresh (15-minute intervals during market hours)
